entry.title: Re: Investment Boost: A significant step to promote economic growth
entry.content:
Hi Bino, 

Is this available in Public Cloud? 

Cheers

entry.title: Material can no longer be changed
entry.content:
Issue: Material cannot be changed.

Error: An error is encountered while attempting to change the material in the PO, both manually and via BAPI in SAP.

Details:

  • PO status: Goods Receipt (GR) completed and subsequently reversed.
  • System does not allow modification of the material after GR completion.

entry.title: Re: How to Trigger the ABAP program in the custom busines...
entry.content:
Hello

Create a custom ABAP class/method exposed as a released API
Wrap it using a released job template (BAdI / RAP behavior / service binding)

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud EWM – Return Excess Subcontracti...
entry.content:
Hi Chris,

Thank you for the information.

We have already tested the return using movement type 542 through MIGO and it works as expected. Our question is specifically from the EWM perspective.

Are there any standard EWM apps or processes available to handle the receipt and putaway of components returned from subcontractor stock after the 542 posting in an EWM-managed warehouse?

entry.title: Re: 10.000 filtermember limit in Data Analyzer filter by...
entry.content:
Hello @MZB 

Yes,10,000 member limit is a standard SAC Data Analyzer UI restriction.
No, there is no SAP Note, RSADMIN setting, or config to increase this limit.


Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: Scoped Role Migration from One Tenant to Another Tenant
entry.content:
Hello

Currently, SAP Datasphere does NOT support transporting scoped roles between tenants
Raise an SAP Influence Request — this is a known gap with growing demand.


Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 01 - Common types and aspects
entry.content: 33d2139cb69b6bf0293ab17f9512dafb126481972ae0684fdac1fdf80b7cbf2f

entry.title: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 05 - Portable functions
entry.content:
At the CAP-level, support for multiple databases is available (SQLite, Postgres, HANA, H2). This support includes standard operators and standard functions, portable across the database runtimes. There are various functions in this set, across different categories:

  • String (concat, length, trim, ...)
  • Numeric (ceil, floor, round, ...)
  • Aggregate (avg, min, max, ...)
  • Date / Time (date, time, year, ...)

In this last category, what is the name of the function for calculating the number of years between two dates?

This is a question from the June Developer Challenge on CAP Knowledge. And don't forget: always submit your answer as a hash, on its own - read the Taking part section of the intro post for more info. At the end of today, this question will be updated with links to further reading on this topic.

entry.title: Re: Variable pay eligibility based on nonrecurring pay co...
entry.content:
@jrmuela 

Rule 5 : https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/compensation-eligibility-rules-lesser-known-rule-conditions-and-overall/ba-p/13487819

entry.title: Standard Message 06 054 when converting PR to PO
entry.content:
Dear SAP experts,
we are using MRP for creating PR's that should be converted to a PO type NBIC. For that purpose we created a PR type NBIC and expect that PR's will be converted to PO's without issue. However, system is trying to use PO type NB instead and issues an error:
"Doc. type/item cat. NBIC/U (requisition) <-> NB/ (purch. order)".

What are we missing? We set up in SSCUI 102909 for PO type NBIC that it can be created from a PR type NBIC with item category "U". Why system is not doing NBIC - NBIC conversion but forcing NB type instead?

entry.title: Please help me to get my article published.
entry.content:
Hello @jerryjanda @mynynachau 
My blog is still under review (The blog post is currently in moderation and is awaiting review) since last Seven days. Please help me to publish the same.

Published URL for this article

https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-blog-posts-by-members/tan-based-exemption-functionality-in-sap-india-localization-complete-guide/ba-p/14406913

entry.title: Re: Transport route
entry.content:
Good morning, 

I would create:

- Consolidation routes from Dev: 100 to a transport group that contains QaS.200 and QaS.300.

- Delivery route from QaS.XXX, which one 200 or 300?,  to QaS.100

- Delivery route from QaS.100 to Prod: 100

Do you know that using ToC you could test the changes into QaS and only release the transport when the testing is successful without the need to use different clients in QaS?

Best regards,

Dolores

entry.title: Re: Design Question – Compensation vs Variable Pay
entry.content:
Hi @mfpabon23 

You need Variable Pay for this requirement, it won't work in Compensation.

We always position Variable Pay now for anything bonus-related implementation just because if customer varpay process changes over the years they need a full new implementation for Varpay if their initial design was in Comp. 

Thanks

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Impact of transferring Individual Data from Inactive...
entry.content:
Hi @Sugandha_29cap- 

It all depends of the UserID. If you Rehire someone and keep the same exact UserID than before termination, then all the data they had prior to Termination (job info history) comes back to life and can be immediatly used by Variable Pay or Compensation.

If you rehire someone under a NEW userID (which I have seen many customers do in case of transfers between Legal Entities where employees signs a local contract in a new country), then you will need to migrate all the data from the terminated profile to the new profile for Variable Pay to get the full employee history for bonus calculation. 

I've worked with 2 customers in the past that have set eligibility rules where an employee who leaves and comes back within the same fiscal year forfeits the part before Termination from their year-end bonus. I documented how to capture it in Variable Pay here:https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/managing-employees-rehired-during-the-same-fiscal-year-than-termination-in/ba-p/13561646 

Thanks

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Clarification on Outbox Behavior in Multi-Instance Deployments
entry.content: I also want to know the details about exponential backoff for retries..

entry.title: Re: Project Budget Inquiry – Grouping by Project Type in...
entry.content:
Good day @AlvaroFernandezMorales 

Thank you for your question.

In the standard Project Budget Inquiry/Project Budget Report app, if Project Type is available only as a filter and not as an output dimension, then you cannot force the standard app to group the result by Project Type.

So, in simple terms:

  • You can only group by Project Type if the field is available as a dimension in the result layout.
  • If it only available as a filter, the standard inquiry cannot group by it.
  • You cannot automatically add Project Class/Project Type as a grouping field unless it has been exposed in the report or part of the released CDS view used for the report.
  • A custom Z field is only useful if the field can be enabled for the relevant project reporting business context and exposed in the analytical data source.

In my opinion, the faster option is for you to build a custom analytical report:

  1. Make use of View Browser to check released project CDS views.                                  
  2. Check whether Project Type/Project Class is available in the released project master data view.
  3. Join or associate this with project cost, commitment, and actual cost reporting data where supported.
  4. Build the output in Custom Analytical Queries.                                                                                      
  5. Add Project Type as a free characteristic or row field.
  6. Add measures for committed cost and actual cost.
  7. Save it as a custom tile for reporting.

Note that there are some limitation on the custom field extensibility in Project Budget/Cost Report Overview hierarchical apps. See KBA 3126986 for more details.

For budget reporting, also check that budget values are assigned to WBS element. You will find details on Project Budget Reporting logic in KBA 3730202.

To summarise:

  • The standard budget inquiry can filter by project type, but it cannot group by Project Type unless SAP has provided the field as an output dimension. If the field is not available in the result layout or grouping options, you cannot change it directly in the standard app.
  • The recommended next step is to create a custom analytical report with released CDS views. If Project Type or Project Class is available in the released project master data view, use it as a row field and combine it with actual and commitment cost measures. If a custom field is used, first confirm whether it is supported in the relevant project reporting business context, because custom fields are not supported in the Project Budget/Cost Report overview hierarchical apps.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Bringing Analytics to Life with Business Builders – The Hockey Analyst
entry.content: That's a very cool idea @luis_colmenares Hope you can make it!

entry.title: Re: Standard Message 06 054 when converting PR to PO
entry.content: I figured it - NB document type was forced by the system because SSCUI 103426 "Assign Document Type, One-Step Procedure, Underdelivery Tolerance" was not maintained in the system. Strange that this was not included in the setup guide for scope item 5HP for "Intercompany Stock Transfer" that NBIC document type should be added there so that there are no issues with standard processes determining order type.

entry.title: Re: System Conversion to SAP S/4HANA
entry.content: The best practice recommendation is to create a fresh copy of the Production system and use it as the Development (DEV) system for conversion activities.

entry.title: GL Account Synchronization finished successfully but G/L Account did not exist in Productio
entry.content:
We are using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with a 2-system landscape (Quality → Production).

We have a G/L Account (00915001) that previously existed in Production. In the past, its Implementation Status was changed from P to N, and the account was subsequently removed from Production as expected through transport.

Recently, we changed the account back from N to P in the Quality system. In addition, we updated the account description and removed all account blocks. After that, we executed the Synchronize G/L Account Master Data app and received a successful result .

However:

The account is not available in Manage G/L Account Master Data in Production.
The account cannot be found when entering G/L Account in journal entry apps.
Other G/L Accounts in the same synchronization run were transferred successfully.

Has anyone experienced a similar issue?

Is it expected behavior that a G/L Account removed from Production due to Implementation Status = N is not automatically recreated after changing the status back to P and running G/L Account Synchronization?

Any related SAP Notes, KBAs, or documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Issue During SAP Certification Exam – Valuation Area...
entry.content:
Hi,

This system behavior happens, when you perform the task of creating the plant in two steps:

  • define the plant (Enterprise Structure --> Definition --> Logistics General --> Define Plant)
  • copy, delete, check plant (Enterprise Structure --> Definition --> Logistics General --> Copy, delete, check plant; TX EC02))

By creating the plant directly using the copy... transaction (EC02), the plant is being created including valuation area. Afterwards you switch to "define" in order to assign the address.

A workaround to get the valuation area assigned to the plant if missing is,  to delete the assignment of your plant to company code 1010  (Enterprise Structure --> Assignment --> Logistics General --> Assign plant to company code; TX OX18), save, exit, go back in, re-assign.

Good lick for your next attempt and getting your exam attempt restored.

Best regards

Martin

entry.title: Header-to-Item Custom Field Copy in S/4HANA Cloud Service using CRMS4_SERV_CUST_H_MODIFY / I_MODIFY
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2602), I had a Service Management requirement that looked trivial on paper and turned into a nice little tour of the extensibility layers: the user enters a Customer Reference only at the service document header, and that value has to be copied down to a custom field at item level automatically. This post walks through why the obvious BAdIs don't get you there on their own, how to confirm developer extensibility is open to you, and the full working implementation including the gotchas that cost me time.

The requirement

Using key user extensibility (Custom Fields app), I created two text custom fields and exposed them on the Manage Service Contracts app via Adapt UI:

  • Header field Customer Reference on business context Service Header (CRMS4_SERV_H). Its persistence component resolved to YY1_CUSTOMERREFERENCE_SRH.
  • Item field Customer Reference Item on business context Service Item (CRMS4_SERV_I).

Both save and display correctly from the UI. The business rule: the user only maintains the header value, and the item field should mirror it without manual entry.

The BAdIs I planned to use

The enhancement spot for service one-order is ES_S4CRM_1O_EXTENSION, which contains, among others:

  • CRMS4_SERV_CUST_H_MODIFY (Service Document Header Modifications) — importing SERVICEHEADER, SERVICEHEADER_EXTENSION_IN; changing SERVICEHEADER_EXTENSION_OUT.
  • CRMS4_SERV_CUST_I_MODIFY (Service Document Item Modifications) — importing SERVICEHEADER, SERVICEITEM, SERVICEITEM_EXTENSION_IN; changing SERVICEITEM_EXTENSION_OUT.

My first instinct was: read the header value in the item BAdI and write it to the item extension field. Simple.

Why that falls short (and why Custom Logic scope can't bridge it)

The item BAdI receives the standard header structure SERVICEHEADER (type CRMS4S_SERV_H_BADI) — but it does not receive the header extension fields. So inside CRMS4_SERV_CUST_I_MODIFY there is no way to read YY1_CUSTOMERREFERENCE from the header. Conversely, the header BAdI has the header custom field but cannot touch items.

So the value lives in one BAdI and has to be written in another. The standard pattern for that is a small in-memory buffer: the header BAdI stashes the value, the item BAdI reads it. The problem with doing this in key user Custom Logic scope is that you can't create a standalone global class or shared object to hold that state, and custom logic can't reference an arbitrary custom class — so a clean cross-header-to-item copy isn't feasible there. That pushed me to developer extensibility.

Checking the API Hub: is it released for Developer Extensibility?

Older SAP Community threads (2024) noted these BAdIs were released only for key user apps (USE_IN_KEY_USER_APPS). Release status changes by release, so check your own. On the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, open the BAdI (Developer Extensibility → BAdIs) and look at the Release State tiles. On 2602, CRMS4_SERV_CUST_I_MODIFY showed Release State Developer Extensibility: Released (and the same for the header BAdI). That's the green light — verify both the header and item definitions before you start.

The approach: developer extensibility + a static buffer bridge

Three things to build: a buffer class to carry the value across the two BAdI calls, plus an implementing class for each BAdI. The header BAdI runs first in the save sequence and fills the buffer; the item BAdI reads it.

Step 1 — Prerequisites

  • Connect ADT to the Development tenant , not the Customizing tenant. Developer extensibility objects can only be created on Development tenant .
  • Have a customer software component and package enabled for ABAP for Cloud Development.

Step 2 — The buffer class

Create and activate this first, since the implementing classes reference it. The key is the document character UUID (SERVICEDOCUMENTCHARUUID), which exists identically on the header and item BAdI's SERVICEHEADER structure and is stable from creation — unlike the document number, which can be empty for a new document.

 
 
abap
CLASS zcl_serv_custref_buf DEFINITION PUBLIC FINAL CREATE PUBLIC.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS set
      IMPORTING iv_guid  TYPE crms4s_serv_h_badi-servicedocumentcharuuid                iv_value TYPE crms4s_serv_h_incl_eew_ps-yy1_customerreference_srh.
    CLASS-METHODS get
      IMPORTING iv_guid         TYPE crms4s_serv_h_badi-servicedocumentcharuuid      RETURNING VALUE(rv_value) TYPE crms4s_serv_h_incl_eew_ps-yy1_customerreference_srh.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_row,
             guid  TYPE crms4s_serv_h_badi-servicedocumentcharuuid,
             value TYPE crms4s_serv_h_incl_eew_ps-yy1_customerreference_srh,
           END OF ty_row.
    CLASS-DATA gt_buf TYPE SORTED TABLE OF ty_row WITH UNIQUE KEY guid.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_serv_custref_buf IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD set.
    DELETE gt_buf WHERE guid = iv_guid.
    INSERT VALUE #( guid = iv_guid value = iv_value ) INTO TABLE gt_buf.
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD get.
    READ TABLE gt_buf WITH KEY guid = iv_guid INTO DATA(ls).
    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      rv_value = ls-value.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Step 3 — Enhancement implementation and BAdI implementations

In ADT: New → Other ABAP Repository Object → Enhancement → Enhancement Implementation, against enhancement spot ES_S4CRM_1O_EXTENSION. Inside that single enhancement implementation you can host both BAdI implementations:

  1. Add BAdI Implementation for BAdI definition CRMS4_SERV_CUST_I_MODIFY (e.g. name ZUPDATE_CUSTREF_H_TO_I), implementing class ZCL_SERV_CUST_I_MODIFY, "Active implementation" ticked.
  2. Add BAdI Implementation for BAdI definition CRMS4_SERV_CUST_H_MODIFY, implementing class ZCL_SERV_CUST_H_MODIFY.

Tip: in the Implementing Class field, type the class name and use the quick-fix (Ctrl+1 → create implementing class) so ADT generates the class already wired to the BAdI interface and the MODIFY method signature.

Step 4 — The header implementing class (fills the buffer)

Read the value from SERVICEHEADER_EXTENSION_OUT first (what the user entered in this interaction); fall back to SERVICEHEADER_EXTENSION_IN (the existing/persisted value) when OUT is empty. This matters — see the gotchas.

 
 
abap
CLASS zcl_serv_cust_h_modify DEFINITION PUBLIC FINAL CREATE PUBLIC.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_crms4_serv_cust_h_modify.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_serv_cust_h_modify IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD if_crms4_serv_cust_h_modify~modify.
    DATA(lv_value) = serviceheader_extension_out-yy1_customerreference_srh.
    IF lv_value IS INITIAL.
      lv_value = serviceheader_extension_in-yy1_customerreference_srh.
    ENDIF.
    zcl_serv_custref_buf=>set(
      iv_guid  = serviceheader-servicedocumentcharuuid      iv_value = lv_value ).
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Step 5 — The item implementing class (reads the buffer)

 
 
abap
CLASS zcl_serv_cust_i_modify DEFINITION PUBLIC FINAL CREATE PUBLIC.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_crms4_serv_cust_i_modify.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_serv_cust_i_modify IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD if_crms4_serv_cust_i_modify~modify.
    DATA(lv_ref) = zcl_serv_custref_buf=>get( serviceheader-servicedocumentcharuuid ).
    " confirm your item field's exact component name (context suffix) via code completion
    serviceitem_extension_out-yy1_customerreferenitm_sri = lv_ref.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Activate the buffer class first, then both implementing classes.

How H_MODIFY and I_MODIFY are triggered

This is the part that's easy to misjudge:

  • The header MODIFY fires whenever the document is saved/touched.
  • The item MODIFY fires per item, only for items that are created or changed in that save — not for untouched items.
  • In the save sequence, the header MODIFY runs before the item MODIFY. That ordering is exactly what makes the buffer work: header sets, item gets.

Consequences:

  • Create a contract with items and a header reference in the same save → both fire → the value copies.
  • Touch an item on an existing contract → the item BAdI fires for that item, reads the buffer (which the header BAdI filled with the current header value via the OUT/IN logic), and copies.
  • Edit only the header reference on an existing contract → the item BAdI does not fire for untouched items, so they won't resync. You cannot flag items as dirty from the header BAdI in this released set, so this is a design boundary to be aware of, not a bug.

Testing and the gotchas

Use the ADT debugger for this (Custom Logic Tracing is for key user logic, not your Z classes). Set breakpoints in both MODIFY methods and run a save from the Fiori UI.

The gotchas that actually bit me:

  1. There is no GUID field on CRMS4S_SERV_H_BADI. The fields use CDS-style names; the usable key is SERVICEDOCUMENTCHARUUID. Don't use the document number — it can be blank for new documents.
  2. Field component names carry a context suffix. The header field resolved to YY1_CUSTOMERREFERENCE_SRH. The item field will have its own suffix (verify with code completion on serviceitem_extension_out-). Get these exact or it won't compile/copy.
  3. Don't write serviceheader_extension_out = serviceheader_extension_in. That line appears in SAP's sample code and overwrites the user's freshly entered value with the old one.
  4. OUT vs IN. In my first test the buffer row existed with the right UUID but a blank VALUE. The reason: I had only changed a line item, not re-entered the header field, so SERVICEHEADER_EXTENSION_OUT came in empty while the actual value sat in SERVICEHEADER_EXTENSION_IN. Reading OUT with an IN fallback (as in Step 4) fixed it and makes the copy robust whether or not the header was touched in that save.
  5. Edit-only-header limitation (above): if the business needs a later header-only change to resync existing items, this in-transaction pattern won't cover it — you'd need an API-driven update that actually touches the items.

Takeaways

The core lesson is that the service item modify BAdI deliberately doesn't expose header extension fields, so any header-to-item copy needs a bridge across the two BAdI calls — a static buffer keyed by the document UUID, populated in the header BAdI and read in the item BAdI. Add to that the OUT/IN value source, the correct UUID key, the context-suffixed field names, and the per-item trigger behavior, and you have a clean, upgrade-stable solution entirely within developer extensibility.

If you've solved the edit-only-header resync cleanly, I'd be glad to hear how in the comments.

entry.title: Re: When order is in credit block have to keep the confir...
entry.content:
Good day @latha9 

Thank you for your question.

No, you cannot achieve that in Public Cloud by standard configuration.

When a sales order becomes credit blocked, the standard behaviour is for you to remove or reset the confirmed quantities. Note that if the system finds the order has a credit block, it removes the confirmed quantities from the schedule lines. This also affects ATP or BOP evaluation because the blocked order no longer keeps the same confirmed requirement in the normal confirmation logic.

See the following reference for more insights.

3686961 - The sales order item has no confirmed schedule line generated after the BOP run. - SAP for Me

So, in simple terms:

  • Credit blocked sales orders do not keep confirmed quantities as a guaranteed ATP reservation.
  • Keeping confirmed quantity while the order remains credit blocked is not supported as a standard behaviour in Public Cloud.
  • Though SAP provides that option, however, it is only available in either on-premise or the Private Cloud environment. Which means the solution is a system modification which is not applicable for Public Cloud. See KBA 2306970 for more details.
  • In Public Cloud, you cannot modify the standard requirement routine to keep the confirmed quantity during credit block.

I recommend the following options:

  1. Release the credit block in your system first, then rerun ATP or BOP.
  2. If the business wants to reserve stock before credit approval, review whether the process can use an approval step before order confirmation instead of allowing the order to become credit blocked after confirmation.
  3. If your requirement is mandatory, you may consider raising an improvement request because this is not available through standard configuration.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 05 - Portable functions
Impairment Posting Error After Missing Fiscal Year Balance Carryforward
entry.content:
Hi Community,

We are facing an issue with impairment posting in Asset Accounting on
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Background:
The fiscal year-end closing was not executed for the past two fiscal years,
and as a result, the balance carryforward was not completed for those years.

Issue:
When attempting to post an impairment charge at the end of the current
fiscal year (net book value: 1 JPY → 0 JPY), an error occurred and the
posting could not be executed through the standard procedure.
This process had been working normally until the month prior to the
fiscal year-end.

We believe the missing balance carryforward for the past two fiscal years
is the root cause of this error.

Questions:
1. Is the missing fiscal year balance carryforward the confirmed root cause
of the impairment posting error?
2. Is re-executing the fiscal year-end closing for the past two years the
only resolution?
3. Are there any known workarounds that avoid re-executing the year-end
closing?

Any insights or experiences from the community would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Development Tools for Visual Studio Code
entry.content:
What is the reason for not adding the capability of editing SAP Reports and Programs. Is this planned for the later version of ADT for Vscode?

thank you,

Harsha

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 05 - Portable functions
entry.content: Interesting challenge that encourages deeper exploration of CAP database portability features. Understanding date and time functions is highly relevant in real SAP applications where reporting, analytics, and lifecycle calculations depend on consistent behavior across supported databases, helping improve maintainability and reducing database specific implementation effort.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 03 - Declarative field control with @mandatory

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 04 - Namespaces, contexts and name prefixes
Re: Title: Residual Delivered Value Remains in PO History...
entry.content:
Hello!

You can try to follow the steps in the KBA 3262805 - Goods Receipt value for a zero Goods Receipt Quantity in the Purchase Order history – Practical Case.

Normally, if you repeat the same postings for your purchase order in the same sequence in a test system, you get to the same final valuation, so if you recreate the same values in a development environment, you can test the solution in the KBA there.

I hope this helps you!
Esther.

entry.title: Re: Impairment Posting Error After Missing Fiscal Year Balance Carryforward
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: GL Account Synchronization finished successfully but G/L Account did not exist in Productio
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: Only Run Advance Foreign Currency Valuation for RE doc type.
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: Impairment Posting Error After Missing Fiscal Year Ba...
entry.content:
Hi,

In Asset Accounting, you are only allowed to open a new fiscal year as long as no more than two fiscal years remain open at the same time.

Example: Let's say the last closed fiscal year for a company code and ledger is 2024. This means both 2025 and 2026 are currently open — which accounts for the maximum of two allowed open fiscal years.

In this situation, attempting to open 2027 will not be permitted and will result in the following error message:

> "Fiscal year change in co.code xxxx possible only after year-end closing 2025."

To proceed, you must first complete the year-end closing for 2025, which will reduce the number of open fiscal years and allow 2027 to be opened.

Kind Regards,
Markus

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 02 - Content for common reuse types
Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 05 - Portable functions
Re: Is there a way to control TECO using the Authorization Key along with the Phase Control BAdI?
entry.content: Hello, by default, the system performs a specific authorization check when a phase control code is activated or deactivated even with BAdI EAM_PHASE_CONTROL_ACTIVATION. So not sure what you mean authorization key is not available within the BAdI. Can you provide more details of your use case and custom code?

entry.title: *Production Order Cost Settlement – Variance Ledger Not Zeroing
entry.content: After running production order settlement, I see 3 line items posted to the variance G/L account instead of the expected 2, and the account is NOT netting to zero.

entry.title: Re: how to custom agent for sales price approval
entry.content:
Good day @fptappuser 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your screenshots, the issue is most likely that the workflow step is still determining recipients only by the function “Sales Price Approver Level 1”, and not by your custom responsibility field Price Channel or your custom creator-based rule.

The standard setup for sales price approval uses Manage Sales Price Workflow, Manage Teams and Responsibilities, and My Inbox. The workflow sends approval tasks to the approvers determined from the workflow step recipient setup and the team responsibility setup.

To put that in more simple terms:

  • If both teams contain users with Sales Price Approver Level 1 function, and the workflow step does not restrict the recipient determination by your custom responsibility value, then all Level 1 approvers can receive the task.
  • Creating a custom responsibility definition such as Price Channel does not automatically make the sales price workflow use it.
  • The workflow must have a supported context field that can pass the value A or B into responsibility determination.
  • If Price Channel is not part of the standard sales price workflow context, the system will ignore it for agent determination.
  • Note that for creator-based approval, the same rule applies. If the creator is not a supported field in the workflow context or in a released sales price workflow BAdI, the workflow cannot automatically route Creator A to Approver B.

For Scenario 1, i suggest you keep to the supported standard design and split the workflow using available workflow start conditions or step conditions, for example condition type, sales organisation, distribution channel, division, or other fields that are exposed in Manage Sales Price Workflows.

For Scenario 2, creator-based approver determination is only possible if the sales price workflow provides a released custom logic or BAdI context that supports this determination. If your custom logic is not called, or the returned approver is not used by the workflow step, then this is not supported for this workflow scenario.

Basically, this means:

  • Your team setup itself may be correct; the issue is that the workflow recipient determination is too broad.
  • The workflow is reading all users assigned to Sales Price Approver Level 1.
  • Your custom responsibility field is not influencing the workflow runtime.
  • To make it work, you must split the workflow using standard workflow conditions or a released enhancement for sales prices approver determination must be provided.

My recommendation is:

Option 1, use standard workflow conditions by creating separate workflows for each approval route, e.g.,

  • Workflow 1: Price Channel A equivalent condition; Workflow 2: Price Channel B equivalent condition

However, this only works if the field you use for the split is available as a standard condition in Manage Sales Price Workflows.

Option 2 is for you to use standard responsibility fields only. In Manage Teams and Responsibilities, maintain only the standard responsibility definitions that are supported by sales price workflow, for example condition type or sales area related fields if available in your tenant.                                                                                       

Option 3 is for creator-based routing. Check Custom Logic for a released sales price workflow agent determination BAdI. If non exists for this exact workflow scenario, then creator-based routing is not supported as a cloud extension.  

Option 4 is if custom Price Channel is mandatory. Here, I would suggest you raise an improvement request because custom responsibility fields are not automatically consumed by the standard sales price approval workflow.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/46f4119db1dc4826b6f8b3683264680e.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/69ae147536d944128b0cf2a99f605f39.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: What are the potential differences in Sales Revenue of Actual Amount and Recognized Revenue?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

We are planning to introduce the Project Financial Booklet – Professional Services app to our business users. To support this transition, I created a bookmark that is similar to the current public bookmark used in the Projects Baseline/EAC/Ongoing app (old version of the Project Finance Booklet).

Since the Project Financial Booklet app does not provide an Actual Amount field, I have used Recognized Revenue instead. So far, the values displayed appear to be the same as the Sales Revenue values under Actual Amount in the Projects Baseline/EAC/Ongoing app.

However, because we need to explain this change to our business users, we would like to understand whether there are any differences in the definition and/or data source between these two measures.

If Sales Revenue (Actual Amount) and Recognized Revenue can produce different values, could you please explain under what circumstances such differences may occur?

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: Re: Error in payment proposal creation
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: Cannot migrate AuC during fiscal year
entry.content: Hi Paaa, you must use Asset Transaction Type 900. BR, M.

entry.title: In SAP Analytics cloud need a report which shows the permissions added in a role

entry.title: Re: Only Run Advance Foreign Currency Valuation for RE do...
entry.content:
Dear @S0027059524 , 

first of all, many thanks for raising this question. 

The document type RE used for Invoice - Goods Receipt as part of the GR/IR process is not beeing covered yet. This is a known feature request to SAP and is already considered in the roadmap of the Advanced Valuation Runs. 

Thanks for your understanding. 

Best,

Kevin

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 05 - Portable functions

entry.title: [Performance History] it cannot show ranking label instead of number
entry.content:
Hi there,

Short Description / Summary:

After upgrading to the latest version of the Employee Profile (People Profile), the Performance History block only displays numeric scores (e.g., "5") instead of rating labels (e.g., "Exceeds Expectations"). We request SAP to restore the option to display text-based rating labels to improve user experience.

Business Case / Description:

Following our recent upgrade to the latest version of the Employee Profile, we noticed a significant regression in the Performance History section.

  • Previous Behavior (Legacy Version): The system allowed us to configure and display text-based rating labels(e.g., "Exceeds Expectations") rather than just a raw number.

  • Current Behavior (Latest Version): The block now strictly displays numeric ratings (e.g., "5").

Why this is an issue: Displaying only numbers is highly counterintuitive and creates an unfriendly user experience for both employees and managers. Users often do not remember what each number stands for, forcing them to manually look up the rating scale mapping. This change has led to unnecessary confusion and extra administrative inquiries for our HR team.

Suggested Solution:

We kindly request SAP to bring back the parity from the previous version. Please provide a configuration option or enhancement in the latest Employee Profile that allows organizations to choose whether to display numeric scores, text rating labels, or both in the Performance History block.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 03 - Declarative field control with @mandatory
Re: SAP S/4HANA Simulator — Practice SPRO and FI/SD trans...
entry.content:
Hi FranciscoIacono,
UKM_MONITOR exist for Update Entries for External Credit Information.
However UKM_RELEASE does not exist as t-code or report. Not sure for which purpose you want to use it.
UKM_CASE can be used by different applications, like credit management, dispute etc. You have to select the correct RMS id and the customizing behind each process has to be in place.
Regards,
Emöke, SAP

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 05 - Portable functions
Enhance Clock In / Clock Out UX with a One-Click Direct Home Page Widget
entry.content:
Hi there,

Short Description / Summary:

The current Clock In / Clock Out (CICO) functionality in SuccessFactors requires too many clicks and navigation steps, making it unintuitive for daily use. We propose a dedicated, static Home Page widget/card that allows employees to clock in, clock out, or go on break with a single click, showing their live status at a glance.

Business Case / Description:

Clocking in and out is a daily, high-frequency action for standard employees. However, the current SuccessFactors UI requires users to navigate through multiple menus or handle pop-up windows just to register a timestamp.

This lack of intuitiveness often leads to:

  • Forgotten punches: Employees forget to clock in/out because the status isn't prominently visible.

  • Wasted time: Navigating the system daily for a simple action creates cumulative productivity loss.

  • High error rates: Users occasionally select the wrong time event type due to non-intuitive dropdown designs.

Proposed Intuitive UI Layout (Concept):

We request SAP to design a streamlined, direct-action card on the Reimagined Home Page that resembles the following layout:

Key Features of this Proposed UI:

  1. Dynamic Status Indicator: Clearly displays whether the user is currently "Clocked In", "Clocked Out", or "On Break" using color-coded text/icons.

  2. One-Click Execution: Clicking "Clock In" instantly registers the timestamp based on local system time withoutopening additional confirmation pop-ups.

  3. Smart Button Toggling: Buttons automatically grey out or activate based on the current state (e.g., if already clocked in, the "Clock In" button becomes unclickable).

Suggested Solution:

Provide a dedicated, high-visibility standard card for the SuccessFactors Home Page specifically for Time Tracking / CICO users, minimizing administrative overhead and maximizing employee adoption.

entry.title: SAP BAS Deployment Issue - Request for Support
entry.content:
Hi Team

Requirement

Need to deploy SAP Fiori application from Business Application Studio (BAS) to ABAP system using deployment configuration.

Environment

SAP Business Application Studio (BAS)

Node.js environment

Fiori tools (@sap/fiori)

Target: ABAP

Steps Performed

1. Open BAS terminal

2. Navigate to project directory

3. Run deployment configuration command

CLI Command Used

npm run deploy-config

Observed Behavior

While adding deployment configuration, system throws error during ABAP target selection.

Error Details

Request failed with status code 404

TypeError [ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE]: The "code" argument must be of type number. Received type string ('ERR_BAD_REQUEST')

Expected Behavior

Deployment configuration should be added successfully and allow deployment to ABAP system.

Additional Notes

System prompts to install @SAP/generator-fiori globally, however issue persists.

entry.title: Re: 103825 - Definir documentos de cambio, solicitud de compra colectiva, relevancia para MRP.
entry.content:
SAP Machine Translation Portal translation:

103825 - Define Change Documents, Collective Purchase Requisition, MRP Relevance.

Good afternoon:

Someone knows why in configuration line 103825 - Define Change Documents, Collective Purchase Requisition, MRP Relevance. I don't get the option to Add a new record?

I get this option disabled even though I have all permissions

Davycruz_0-1780610967659.png

in other configuration lines if I get the option enabled without problem.

Greetings.

entry.title: Re: Integration suite API that retrieves the message size
entry.content:
The MessageProcessingLogs API is the correct monitoring API, but it does not provide payload size natively. Therefore, implementing a Groovy step that calculates the payload size and stores it as a Custom Header Property is the recommended and commonly used approach for making payload size available through the MPL OData API.

SAP help document: Get Custom Header Properties | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: SAP Incentive Management: Keeping track of Payment Attributes

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Integration into Joule
entry.content:
Hello @AshrafMohammed 

i noticed in a new Setup that Work Zone Channel not accpet a RT Destination which has ProxyType Internet, as it was before, not sure if that is a new feature...

when i change the RT dest to ProxyType on Prem, it is selectable, but that means the dest has http instead of https and also for sure will not work as the Virtual Ip not present somewhere.

For sure i can manipulate the dest afterwards again to the old stuff but then the question comes in any updates or change moment it will again disappear in channel setup...

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 02 - Content for common reuse types
購買発注(ME21N)の「個人設定」の初期値が設定されてしまっている
entry.content:
SAPサポートご担当者様

以下2点について内容のご確認と対応方法のご教授をいただきたく思います。
①購買発注(ME21N)の「個人設定」の初期値が意図せず登録されており、想定と異なる購買組織で発注する動きとなる。
⇒「個人設定」の初期値が設定された理由をご教授いただきたい。

②SU01のユーザの変更文書からパラメータの変更履歴を確認することができない。
⇒ユーザのパラメータ変更履歴の確認方法をご教授いただきたい。

以上2点ご確認のほどよろしくお願いいたします。

entry.title: Error "Space Version of space <SPACEID> is too low to run transformation flow" in Datasphere ?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

we encounter this error today that stop all the Task chains in Datasphere.

 

 

 

The resolution explained by the note https://me.sap.com/notes/3727880/E

is "deploy the transformation flow again"...

 

So we have a random error,

the causes are not explained (so it is not predictable / avoidable),

the solution is a manual activity (potentially apply to a hundred of objects).

in our case the transformation flow that goes into an error is just "1 month old".

And there is no explicit committment from SAP to solve this issue in the note.

 

@ experts, What do you think about this sitution?

@kpsauer What do you think ?

entry.title: Re: 103825 - Definir documentos de cambio, solicitud de c...
entry.content:
Hello,

Configuration '103825 - Define Change Documents, Collective Purchase Requisition, MRP relevance' does not support user to create new entries in Public Cloud.

Once you have defined new Plant in your organizational structure in CBC, the Plant and Order Type combinations should be enabled in the configuration automatically.

Best Regards,

Nick

entry.title: BAdI for Filter in Retail Fiori app Order Products
entry.content:
The Retail Fiori App Order Products (F0752) can be extended with custom filters using BAdI's BADI_RTST_OP_FILTER and BADI_RTST_OP_FILTERVALUE. This works fine; filters and their values appear in the app. When selecting a filter value however, the app breaks with message "Property x not found in type StoreProduct" because the filter field is not available in the metadata of the service. The app is based on SEGW service RETAILSTORE_ORDER_PRODUCT.  

The documentation off these BAdI's gives the following information:
For each new filter, a new corresponding property has to be created in the entity StoreProduct of the OData Service RETAILSTORE_ORDER_PRODUCT_SRV. The name of the property has to correspond to the ID of the filter so that the UI can map the filter value to the respective property. A new field also has to be created in the structure RTST_OP_S_STORE_PRODUCT_INCL that must be mapped to the new property in the entity.

There seems to be no BAdI in the SEGW service itself to add the property so how can the property be added in a clean way? Creating a custom service to redefine the standard service appears to be the only way to manage custom filters. Or am I overlooking an option?

entry.title: Re: Functional Currency Transactions
entry.content:
Hi Michal, 

Thanks for your detailed answer, I understand the logic now and we'll proceed with any of the options you suggest we'll need to check which suits our customer best. 

Kind Regards, Sanne

entry.title: Re: Requirement: Automated Detection of Partition Failures in Replication Flow
entry.content:
Hello @rajib_chakrabortyd68cbe 
So far I have not yet taken care to check for partition-level failures 🤔
How exactly do you do this?

Should not a failure be repeated automatically, as long as the status is retrying and not cancelled?

Out of interest... do you have all relevant notes in place, in your source system?

You can use SE38 with program CNV_NOTE_ANALYZER_DI to check if and which notes are missing and can be implemented.

Maybe this helps to stabilize your replication flows?

entry.title: Re: Display of all business objects for maintenance order...
entry.content:
Hi,

this requires a special development, or alternatively you can use an existing SAP add-on such as the YAC Digital FIle Manaer by Znk - YAC Digital File Manager — Maintenance Work Packs in a Few Clicks | ZNK

entry.title: Can Tax Calculation in Expense Reports be restricted to specific Expense Types?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are currently investigating the Expense Report functionality in SAP Business ByDesign.

We found that VAT/Tax-related processing in Expense Reports appears to be available through the Tax Activation - Generic Country/Region Version configuration activity ("Activate Tax Calculation").

Our customer has the following requirement:

  • Only certain expense types are eligible for VAT claims.
  • Other expense types should not be subject to VAT processing.
  • Therefore, they would like to know whether tax-related processing (such as Tax Code selection or tax calculation) can be enabled only for specific Expense Types.

Example:

  • Taxi Expense → VAT applicable
  • Hotel Expense → VAT applicable
  • Employee Allowance → VAT not applicable
  • Mileage Claim → VAT not applicable

We would like to understand:

  1. After Tax Calculation is activated for Expense Reports, can Tax Code selection be restricted to specific Expense Types only?
  2. Is it possible to define certain Expense Types as VAT-relevant and others as non-VAT-relevant within standard ByDesign functionality?
  3. If this is not supported, what is the recommended standard approach to handle situations where only some employee expenses are eligible for VAT claims?
  4. Are there any known limitations or best practices regarding Tax Activation in Expense Reports?

We have reviewed KBA 2546221 regarding Tax Activation for Expense Reports, but could not find information about Expense-Type-specific control. (https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2917069)

Any experience or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: User Filtering for Repository Synchronization in SAP Identity Access Governance
entry.content: Very detailed explanation of sync..

entry.title: Re: Purchasing Document and Purchasing Document Item Not...
entry.content: This is not a missing setting. That is how the app is designed. Use other apps or CDS views to get PO references. For PO-related analysis, rely on Process Flow or Journal Entries apps rather than Supplier Line Items.

entry.content: Custom development or add-on, see here https://znk.lu/en/case-studies/yac-digital-file-manager-work-packs - also works with invoices

entry.title: Need Assistance in Checking the Feasibility of Increasing the Decimal Values.
entry.content:
In transaction code AR29N (Revaluation/New Valuation of Assets), under the tab Index-Based New Valuation, we have a requirement to increase the decimal precision. Currently, the system appears to allow only 2 decimal places.

For example:
Index Value: 10.34321

  However, in the Index for New Valuation of Assets field under the Index Base New Valuation tab, the system only accepts 10.34 instead of 10.34321 which results deviation in expected output.

Could you please provide a solution to increase the decimal count for this field?

 

Thanks & Regards,

Yash K

entry.title: Re: Portugal – Support for Original / Duplicate / Triplicate Identification in Billing Document Outp
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: Secondary Cost G/Ls Not Visible in Project Costs App
entry.content:
Good day @rohini_dutta21 

Thank you for your question.

Please be aware that the project cost does not simply display every posting that is visible in Display Line Items. The G/L account must be correctly assigned in Financial Statement Version YPS2, and the related financial statement item must be assigned to the correct semantic tag, such as ACT_COST. See KBA 3453098 for more details.

To put that in simple terms:

  • If a secondary cost G/L account is posted to the project but not mapped correctly in YPS2, it may not appear in the Project Costs app.
  • Display Line Items shows journal entry line items, so it can show more details than the analytical project cost app.
  • It is not correct to say that YPS2 must only contain Primary Cost and Revenue G/L accounts.
  • Secondary cost G/L accounts can be included if they are relevant for project actual cost reporting and are assigned under the correct YPS2 node.
  • Do not add secondary cost accounts randomly. Only add the relevant secondary cost elements that should be treated as project costs in the report.
  • After changing YPS2, run Replicate Runtime Hierarchy so that the report can consume the updated hierarchy.

A useful point is that in Public Cloud, secondary cost elements are also G/L accounts with account type Secondary Cost, used for internal value flows such as allocations.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c56f622a2edf491b9f1b596b55587009/13b27f3ce6d44b6888c53244eaa4581d.html?locale=en-US

In summary:

The behaviour you see is standard. The Project Costs app depends on the reporting structure and semantic tag assignment in YPS2, while Display Line Items shows the journal entry postings directly. If secondary cost G/L accounts should be included in project cost reporting, they must be assigned to the correct node in YPS2, and the related financial statement item must be assigned to ACT_COST. After the change, run Replicate Runtime Hierarchy and test the report again.  

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Customizing of user-defined statuses for production orders in S4 Hana Public Cloud

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 05 - Portable functions
Re: A Practical Approach to Automating External Parameter Changes in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content: Thank you both @venkatamandavilli  and @HarshPatelV, for your feedback. This is a grey area in many projects, where there is often an assumption that it is not a concern. In reality, however, these configurations carry significant responsibility, and without proper setup and governance, the entire SAP ecosystem can become vulnerable to failures.

entry.title: Re: Summarize values for total amount of rows
entry.content: Hi Jessica, you get an absolutely expected result for the formula you are performing. Measure "Count" = count of your records (9061). If you need a total amount of hours you need to use it as a main measure, and not count. You can assign it to be a measure in the query (semantic type measure instead of dimension).

entry.title: Re: Conversational Analytics on SAP S/4HANA CDS View Data with AI Claude Code and SAP BusinessObject
entry.content: Thank you Venkat for your kind feedback!. Appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts.

entry.title: India GST 5% tax codes are missing in S/4HANA Cloud Public standard tax code list.
entry.content:
We are currently implementing the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and preparing the GST tax code setup. 

Below GST rates are missing 

- 5% GST: CGST 2.5% + SGST 2.5%, or IGST 5%
- 18% GST: CGST 9% + SGST 9%, or IGST 18%

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: India GST 5% tax codes are missing in S/4HANA Cloud P...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

India is enabled for TDT (Time-Dependent Tax) Solution. Time Dependent Taxes (TDT) enables you to create tax codes, edit Tax Code, change tax rates with validity periods to your existing tax codes in the event of a legal change.

Please follow the resolution steps provided in ##0003763311 - GST Tax Codes for India are Missing From the Standard Tax Code List.

You are able to create your own tax codes according to the legal changes. 

For detailed steps on how to create tax code, you can refer to the KBA "3009061 - How to create tax code in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition" 

Regards, 

Arjun

entry.title: Don’t Miss It: SAP BTP for Beginners This Monday
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I know it’s already Friday and the weekend is just around the corner, but here’s a quick reminder 😊

Our SAP BTP for Beginners live session is happening this Monday 📅 June 8

To give you more flexibility, we’re offering 2 sessions:

- 🕘 09:00 (CEST)

- 🕓 16:00 (CEST)

 

💡 Please note: The sessions will be held in English.

I’ll be sharing more information about upcoming German live sessions in the next few days.

My colleague Michael Schedrin will guide you through the session and provide a solid introduction to SAP BTP, covering key concepts and practical guidance to help you get started.

If you're new to SAP BTP or looking to strengthen your understanding of the fundamentals, this session is a great place to begin.

What to expect:

- Introduction to SAP BTP

- Overview of key services and concepts

- Practical guidance for getting started

 

👉 Save your spot here: 

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/a-guide-to-sap-btp-for-complete-beginners?searchId=e7747116-6683-4669-a157-f941a15591bd&listPosition=1

 

Looking forward to seeing many of you there!

Best regards,

Isabella

entry.title: Re: Issue in BAPI_SALESORDER_SIMULATE for mass number of...
entry.content: Which error message lacks sufficient information? Do previous informational messages (e.g., of type 'Information') include the item reference or an item number?

entry.title: Re: Building a Cost Center Hierarchy Analytical Model in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: Excellent Blog.

entry.title: Re: Purchasing Document and Purchasing Document Item Not...
entry.content:
Dear @vivekvyas,

Thank you for raising this query.

Yes, this is standard and expected system behavior. In the Manage Supplier Line Items app, the Purchasing Document and Purchasing Document Item columns are available in the report layout; however, they will typically appear blank or show a default value of "00000" for most supplier invoice line items. This is by design and is documented in the SAP Help:
[Manage Supplier Line Items: FAQ]

The reason for this behavior is rooted in how purchase orders and supplier invoices are structured:
Multiple purchase orders can be consolidated into a single invoice. Since there is generally exactly one line item per invoice, the system is unable to establish a unique, unambiguous link back to a specific purchase order at the line item level — and therefore cannot display the Purchasing Document details in this context.

There is no configuration or setting that will enable these values to be populated for standard invoice line items, as this is a fundamental system design behavior and not a gap or defect. If you need to identify document relationships, you can consider the following alternatives:

  • Add the "Original Reference Document" and "Reference Document Type" columns to the table in the Manage Supplier Line Items app to identify the supplier invoice number.
  • Use the Manage Journal Entries app and navigate to the Related Documents tab to get a complete overview of all documents related to a specific line item.

A valid question is why the Purchasing Document and Purchasing Document Item columns exist in the app if they are generally not populated. The reason is that there are exceptional use cases where these fields can be filled. A key example is the Supplier Down Payment Requests. When a down payment request is created for a supplier, the fields Purchasing Document and Purchasing Document Item can be explicitly maintained. In such cases, the values will be displayed correctly in these columns.

Best regards
Steffen

entry.title: Live session: SAP EHS Risk Assessment in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud | June 10, 2026 | 15:00 CET
entry.content:
Most companies don't have a safety problem. They have a visibility problem.

Risks tracked in spreadsheets. Safety data scattered across emails and disconnected tools. Hazard assessments that are outdated, buried, or missing entirely. And when something finally goes wrong, the uncomfortable truth often surfaces: the risk was known. It just wasn't visible in time to act on it.

This is exactly what SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud for EHS solves.

On June 10 at 15:00 CET, LeverX and SAP are hosting a free one-hour live session on EHS Risk Assessment with a hands-on demo showing how structured safety processes in SAP S/4HANA can transform reactive firefighting into genuine preventive control.

What you'll see

In 60 minutes, you'll discover how to identify and assess risks before they turn into incidents, structure safety processes consistently across your organization, and ensure compliance through clear, auditable workflows. The session includes a live demo covering chemical approval workflows, Operational Risk Assessment, Job Hazard Analysis, safety instructions for equipment and substances, and workplace safety analytics; all within standard SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud functionality

Who should attend

This session is designed for EHS and HSE professionals seeking a structured approach beyond spreadsheets, risk and compliance managers, operations and plant managers who need real-time visibility, sustainability and ESG leaders, SAP and IT architects, and quality or process improvement managers embedding safety into operations.

Your speakers

Vladimir Voronov, SAP EHS & Sustainability Delivery Lead at LeverX, and Leyi Liu, Product Marketing Manager at SAP, will guide you through the session and take your questions live.

Event details

Date: June 10, 2026 Time: 15:00 CET Duration: 1 hour Language: English Registration: Risk Assessment & Workplace Safety in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

entry.title: Re: Requirement for Project-wise Customer and Supplier Li...
entry.content: Dear Sivakumar, with the given information it is hard to determine the root cause. Please compare the sample code of the blog post with the implementation on your side. In case you feel that the implemented substitution logic should apply the correct value to your custom field, please raise an ServiceNow case which would allow us to analyze the issue in detail. Best regards, Steffen
